safety_use_cases.label: PPE compliance monitoring
safety_use_cases.description: Use camera analytics to detect when teams remove required PPE when unobserved and address it proactively rather than punitively.

evidence.quote: And by the Big Brother side of it is, I need to know if we have a cell of people that as soon as the door's shut, they're not wearing their PPE.
So I can more organically address that.
Well, and I think that we do have a culture of cameras here already.
Yeah, I mean, that's exactly where ergonomics, forklift safety, PPE detection, obstructions, we call it no pedestrian zones, areas that you don't want people to be

safety_use_cases.label: Ergonomics detection and real-time coaching (reduce injuries and costs)
safety_use_cases.description: Recognize poor ergonomics and lifting techniques and enable near real-time coaching to reduce numerous ergonomic injuries and associated costs.

evidence.quote: it seems like I remember you had the abilities to recognize. We poor ergonomics, poor lifting techniques.
We have a lot of ergonomic issues here, and we have a lot of manual work here.
And just being able to call that out and do corrections almost in real time would be fantastic.
I will also tell you that a large share of those cases are nonspecific ergonomic injuries that resulted in work restrictions or stronger treatment.
So, knocking out several injuries that are from poor ergonomics or questionable ergonomics is not cheap, right? That saves us money, so that would be good.

safety_use_cases.label: Forklift/PIT and pedestrian safety monitoring (including no pedestrian zones)
safety_use_cases.description: Monitor powered industrial truck operations and enforce no pedestrian zones to reduce vehicle-related risks across multiple forklift types.

evidence.quote: Yeah, I mean, that's exactly where ergonomics, forklift safety, PPE detection, obstructions, we call it no pedestrian zones, areas that you don't want people to be
Lots of different styles, too.
So we'll have walk behinds.
We'll have walk behind stackers, forklifts.
Stand-up lifts, sit-down lifts.
We have a lot of vehicles.

safety_use_cases.label: EHS management software adoption
safety_use_cases.description: Revisit procuring an EHS management software once budgets are released to improve safety management processes.

evidence.quote: It sounds like a lot of the stuff we had on hold that would have been ancillary and not necessarily productivity-related is going to be back on the table.
So I had been on hold for an EHS management software.

safety_use_cases.label: Camera enhancements for safety oversight (Voxel-like)
safety_use_cases.description: Evaluate camera enhancements similar to Voxel to expand oversight and safety monitoring when capital becomes available.

evidence.quote: I'd been on hold for some sort of camera enhancements similar to what you – exactly to what you guys are doing because I've seen your product and I like it.
There would be a lot of benefit to having more oversight.

We have a lot of ergonomic issues here, and we have a lot of manual work here.
And just being able to call that out and do corrections almost in real time would be fantastic.
I will also tell you that a large share of those cases are nonspecific ergonomic injuries that resulted in work restrictions or stronger treatment.
So, knocking out several injuries that are from poor ergonomics or questionable ergonomics is not cheap, right? That saves us money, so that would be good.

nonsafety_use_cases.label: Build business case and drive cultural/retention benefits with Voxel
nonsafety_use_cases.description: Develop a business case for Voxel that quantifies ROI from reduced injury costs and leverages Voxel to improve culture and employee retention.

evidence.quote: I'm just transparently asking just because, obviously, one part of it is helping you guys build out a business case, too.
there's ROI in multiple aspects of it from an operational standpoint, from just a culture standpoint in general.
I think there's definitely ways for Voxel to help in that standpoint and improving employee retention.
maybe how can we make it more positive and how can we leverage Voxel to do that and get people that want to come to work, you know?
there's a cost associated with every single injury we have, and none of them are insignificant.
